schema_output.narrative_text: Ḥāṭib ibn Abī Balṭah reported to the Prophet about his conversation with al-Muqawqis, the ruler of Alexandria, who promised to send a delegation to support the Prophet.
schema_output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor storybook illustration, soft warm hand-painted style for ages 6-12. A simple 7th century room in Madinah with unpainted mud-brick walls in warm earth tones and a packed-earth floor partly covered by a woven palm-fiber mat. Soft evening sunlight streams through an open wooden window on the left, casting warm rays across the room. In the center sits a cushion on the floor, and on it sits the central figure, dressed in his traditional attire, wearing a flowing white thobe with a distinctive deep green woolen cloak draped over both shoulders and a white turban with a cloth tail hanging between the shoulder blades. Beside him stands Ḥāṭib ibn Abī Balṭah, a slender older man with slightly stooped shoulders, wearing an off-white thobe and a plain brown woolen cloak with a white cloth head covering. Ḥāṭib is speaking to the central figure with a serious expression, his mouth slightly open as if sharing a story. Soft warm golden light, intimate and solemn mood. Medium shot from slightly above. Warm beige, cream, and golden palette with the deep green cloak as a color anchor. No modern objects, historically accurate 7th century scene.
